summary: Send a transactional SMS through a contract-stable, vendor-neutral interface — E.164 recipient, a plain-text body, transient-retry, typed errors — with pluggable, attested vendor adapters.

exports: class SmsError extends Error { code: SmsErrorCode; retryable: boolean; status: number | null }

invariants: Importing performs no I/O and never throws; configuration is validated at call time with typed errors.
An invalid message fails fast with a typed error and zero network calls (recipient must be E.164, body non-empty).
Disallowed control characters in the body or sender are rejected before any send.
Transient vendor failures (429, 5xx, network) are retried up to 3 attempts with backoff + jitter; permanent failures are never retried.
All failures surface as typed SmsError values; raw vendor responses never escape the part.
Secret values never appear in error messages.
notes: The SMS twin of email.transactional and its second worked vendor-flip: twilio (one Basic-auth REST call — the easy/expensive vendor an agent reaches for) vs amazon-sns (SigV4-signed Publish — the fiddly/cheaper one), a one-command flip. Both adapters are zero-dependency (REST + hand-signing, no aws-sdk); the SNS adapter reuses the SES SigV4 signer with service=sns. Stateless (no DB, no migrations). SMS pricing carries carrier/segment fees — quote per-segment with a date stamp, never a hard monthly figure; the flip is one commit in code, but provisioning (Twilio number / SNS sandbox exit + 10DLC/sender-ID registration) stays the founder's. Further adapters (Vonage, MessageBird, Plivo) are interchangeable by construction.
